Patch Set 2:

> @Andreas: I tried "gdb lfs", what issues should I be looking for?

That it works at all.  In the current build tree, gdb will fail because "lfs" is just a wrapper script that launches the .libs/lt-lfs binary with the right LD_LIBRARY_PATH.

Patch Set 2:

>> @Andreas: I tried "gdb lfs", what issues should I be looking for?

> That it works at all.  In the current build tree, gdb will fail because "lfs" is just a wrapper script that launches the .libs/lt-lfs binary with the right LD_LIBRARY_PATH.

Oh, I thought you meant gdb stopped working with James' patch. Ok, yes, the patch fixes that too =)

Patch Set 2:

I use './configure --disable-shared' for local builds to avoid creation of the libtool wrapper scripts.

Patch Set 2:

I am not against leaving things as they are. But is there a benefit to keeping the current behaviour over the one James is suggesting?

parents.subject: LU-11153 quota: initialize ver for default quota

commit.subject: LU-10627 tests: don't use libtool wrapper for applications
commit.message:
LU-10627 tests: don't use libtool wrapper for applications

It is a common pratice of lustre developers to test within the
lustre tree without actually installing lustre onto the local
node. In order for this to work the test suite needs to use
the binary executables instead of the libtool executable wrappers.
Add in the libtool LDFLAG to prevent the creation of the wrappers
for the lustre utils. Additionally properly set LD_LIBRARY_PATH
to where libtool caches the dynamic libraries.

Change-Id: I9570fcb65b927463076f28c47ecec924602bef4e
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32835

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
